Petition For Order Determining Estate Has No Interest In Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Determining Estate Has No Interest In Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-25-2015 Continued To Oct-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Freda Pechner (via court call) for the Commission for Preservation of Pioneer Jewish Cemeteries and Landmarks in the West. Doug Murken for (executor) Blake Weston.

The Court stated that there is an issue regarding proof of service on the Attorney General. Ms. Pechner stated that she is 95% sure she served them but does not have proof, that they are not sure the Attorney General is entitled to notice, that documents sent to the Attorney General and IRS already declare that the property belongs to the Commission, that this petition is just to determine the boundary line, cited P.C. 851(d)(3) stating that this petition is brought by a charitable trust but is actually for an estate, that they could find no cases in Lexis Nexis and that they have no objection to the issue of how to deed to the Commission. The Court stated that it cannot go forward without notice to the Attorney General. Ms. Pechner stated that she will send new notice with a copy of the petition. The petition for order determining estate has no interest in real property was continued to October 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Determining Entitlement To Distribution Under Probate Code 11700, Or In The Alternative , Petition For Instructions Under Probate Code Section 9611.
Off Calendar Jun-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randi Sue Teichman, for and with petitioner Blake Weston (Executor); Jeffrey Schoppert for Guide Dogs for the Blind.
The Court noted that there was no appearance or objection to the petition by Classic Cars.

Mr. Schoppert stated that as no objection was raised to the petitioner's requested relief, he believes that Guide Dogs for the Blind would be entitled to any distribution rather than Classic Cars. Ms. Teichman stated that according to the spreadsheet she has received of the estate's condition, it appears that there will be a deficiency of one million dollars, and that there will be no distribution going to the residuary beneficiary. The Court inquired whether Classic Cars had been informed that there would be no reisduary estate for distribution to the residuary beneficiary. Ms. Teichman replied that she was not sure, but may have so advised them last fall. The Court stated that the petition sought a determination that Guide Dogs for the Blind was the residuary beneficiary, and confirmation that payment of estate taxes was to come from the residue of the estate first. There being no objection, the Court granted the petition for order determining entitlement to distribution (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
